# **Step-by-Step Guide to Separating Alarms into Groups in Talos**

# **Step-by-Step Guide to Separating Alarms into Groups in Talos**

## **Prerequisites (Genesis Setup)**

Before starting the configuration in Talos, the following must be done in Genesis:

1. **Create Customer Groups in Genesis:** Create customer groups (e.g., "all production sites" and "trial sites") to categorize your customer sites.

2. **Assign Sites and Users in Genesis:** Move all production sites into the production customer group and all trial sites into the trial customer group. Current operators must have their access modified to view only the restricted customer groups (e.g., production sites).

## **Talos Configuration Steps**

The configuration in Talos must be done at the company level, typically within the Workflow application.

1. ## **Create Organization Units (OUs) and Assign Sites**

Since customer groups from Genesis do not automatically sync to Talos, organizational units must be created manually to replicate the structure:

1. Log into the **Workflow application**.

   2. Go to **Site Groups**.

   3. Create two new organizational units that correspond to the desired groups (e.g., "prod sites" and "test sites").

   4. Include the corresponding sites or site groups (which are customers in Genesis) into these organizational units.

![][image1]

2. ## **Configure Alarm Channels and Routing**

Alarm channels are placeholders to hold the alarms, and routing rules direct incoming alarms to the correct channel.

1. Navigate to **Company Settings \> Alarm Settings**.

   2. In the **Alarm Channels** section, create new alarm channels corresponding to the groups (e.g., "prod sites" channel).

   3. In the default alarm channel, set up a **routing rule**:

   Add a route (e.g., "to prod channel").

   Set the **Target Channel** (e.g., "prod sites" channel).

   ![][image2] Define the condition: Use the created organizational units. For instance, route any alarm originating from the "PROD sites" organizational unit to the "prod sites channel".

   4. *Alternatively (to minimize setup for sites moving to production):* Create a channel and organizational unit only for test sites. Set a rule that anything **not** from the test site organization group is considered production and routed accordingly.

3. ## **Create Workspaces**

Workspaces are used to bundle one or more alarm channels. Users are assigned to workspaces, not directly to channels.

1. In the **Alarm Settings** section, create a **workspace** (e.g., "pro workspace").

   2. Link the relevant alarm channels to this workspace (e.g., select the "prod sites" channel). This means a user assigned to this workspace will only see alarms from that channel.

4. ## **Configure User Types/Roles**

A restricted user type is created to enforce which workspace a particular user segment (e.g., operators) can access.

1. Navigate to **User Settings \> User Types**.

   2. Create a new user type (e.g., "PROD user type operator").

   3. Configure the role by setting the **White listed workspace** to the restricted workspace (e.g., "prod workspace").

   4. Set the restricted workspace as the **default workspace** for this user type.

   5. Set the **accessible site groups** (e.g., the production organizational unit).

5. ## **Assign User Roles**

The final step is to apply the newly configured restricted user type to the relevant users (e.g., operators).

1. Go to the list of **Users**.

   2. Select the operator login.

   3. Change their role to the restricted user type (e.g., "prod operator").

This restriction ensures that operators will only receive alarms from the production sites, as their user type only grants them access to the associated production workspace. Administrators, by default, will usually maintain access to everything unless their roles are also specifically restricted.

# **Maintenance Consideration**

When moving a site from the test group to the production group, the customer must unlink the site from the test organization unit in Talos (if the test-based configuration approach was used). This process should be documented and shared with the customer.
